title: Gronophyllum procerum
intro: Gronophyllum procerum is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Burseraceae and commonly known as 'Graveolent Storax Tree'. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Gronophyllum procerum include sesquiterpenes, particularly caryophyllene oxide and α-humulene, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, tinctures, and decoctions, which are employed in traditional medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Gronophyllum procerum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. It is a genus of trees native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in countries such as Indonesia and Malaysia. Within this classification, Gronophyllum procerum is a part of the order Malpighiales. The Euphorbiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising over 10,000 species. Gronophyllum procerum is often found growing in well-drained areas, typically at high elevations.
